channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Tariffs will cost families nearly $5k per year, Kentucky governor says
item.description: "That's months upon months upon months of groceries. That's months upon months of rent. That might be your entire annual deductible if you've got private health insurance coverage," he said.
item.dc:creator: Isaiah Calalang, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Autism rates climb to 1 in 31 in kids; Experts debate causes and solutions
item.description: Robert F. Kennedy Jr. has cited research that documents up to a 400% increase in autism cases over the last several decades and is calling for more resources to be devoted to autism studies, but experts want to make sure the lens is pointed in the right direction.
item.dc:creator: Dina Bair, WGN 9

item.title: Actor Michelle Trachtenberg died of complications from diabetes, says NYC medical examiner
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Actor Michelle Trachtenberg died as a result of complications from diabetes, New York City's medical examiner said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: AmeriCorps members who respond to disasters and help nonprofits are let go in DOGE cuts
item.description: DES MOINES, Iowa (AP) — Young volunteers who respond to natural disasters and help with community projects across the U.S. have been discharged as a result of the Trump administration 's campaign to shrink government workforce and services.
item.dc:creator: HANNAH FINGERHUT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Colorado company to become first to refuel Space Force asset
item.description: Astroscale U.S. Inc., a Denver-based on-orbit services and logistics company, will perform two refueling operations of a U.S. Department of Defense satellite.
item.dc:creator: Spencer Kristensen, NewsNation

item.title: California sues to stop Trump from imposing sweeping tariffs
item.description: SACRAMENTO, Calif. (AP) — California Gov. Gavin Newsom sued the Trump administration on Wednesday, challenging the president's authority to impose sweeping tariffs that have set off a global trade war.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIE AUSTIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: MLB ump leaves game after foul ball hits him in the face
item.description: Veteran umpire Hunter Wendelstedt instantly hit the ground after he took a direct hit from the line smash off the bat of New York Mets center fielder Tyrone Taylor in the seventh inning.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: How war, money and the quest for discovery entwined the US government and universities
item.description: The showdown between the Trump administration and Harvard University is spotlighting bare-knuckled politics and big dollar figures.
item.dc:creator: Adam Geller, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Zoom outage impacting users nationwide, service restored according to update
item.description: (KRON) -- Teleconferencing software platform experienced a nationwide outage Wednesday. According to reports on Downdetector.com, users cited problems with the platform's website and app, along with log-in issues.
item.dc:creator: Alex Baker, KRON4

item.title: Family says ICE agents smashed car window in seizing Guatemalan man who's seeking asylum
item.description: NEW BEDFORD, Mass. (AP) — A Massachusetts family is demanding answers from U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement, complaining its agents smashed a car window with a hammer and detained a man who they claim had applied for asylum.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY and RODRIQUE NGOWI, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Zoom service restored after outage affects thousands
item.description: Thousands of Zoom users reported issues accessing virtual meetings Wednesday, with the site confirming shortly after noon it was investigating the issue.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, KTLA 5

item.title: Van Hollen denied meeting, phone call with Abrego Garcia during El Salvador visit
item.description: The Salvadoran government on Wednesday rebuffed Sen. Chris Van Hollen’s (D-Md.) request to meet or speak with a wrongfully deported Maryland man, the senator said, accusing the Trump administration and others of lying about Kilmar Abrego Garcia’s gang ties.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: McDonald's teases the return of the Snack Wrap
item.description: McDonald's has finally teased a re-release date for a once-popular menu item. Kind of. In a relatively cryptic post to X on Tuesday, the fast-food chain announced the beloved Snack Wraps would return on the 14th.
item.dc:creator: Katlyn Fernandez, KTLA 5

item.title: Search continues for 20-year-old Chicagoan who went missing when boat capsized in Wisconsin River
item.description: WISCONSIN DELLS, Wis. (WFRV) - The search is still ongoing for the 20-year-old Chicagoan who went missing after a boat capsized in the Wisconsin River on April 3, with one person escaping and another person being found dead a day later. 20-year-old Francesco L.
item.dc:creator: Adam Rosen, WGN 9

item.title: 10 guards charged over the fatal beating of a New York inmate, including 2 with murder
item.description: Ten prison guards were charged in connection with the fatal beating of a 22-year-old prison inmate, including two charged with murder.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: ACLU sues Defense Department school system over banning race- and gender-related books
item.description: The American Civil Liberties Union has sued the Department of Defense’s school system for children of military families, asserting that the removal of race- and gender-related books and curricula violated students’ First Amendment protections against government censorship.
item.dc:creator: OLIVIA DIAZ, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Menendez brothers' family accuses DA of violating victim protection law after showing graphic photo in court
item.description: Family of Lyle and Erik Menendez are accusing Los Angeles District Attorney Nathan Hochman's office of violating Marsy's Law after graphic crime scene images are shown in court.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Boasberg moves to hold Trump administration in contempt over deportation flights
item.description: A federal judge found probable cause Wednesday to hold the Trump administration in criminal contempt for willfully disobeying his order to immediately halt deportations under the rarely used Alien Enemies Act and turn around any airborne planes. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Company retaliates after asphalt deal goes wrong: Police
item.description: What was supposed to be a typical paving job culminated in a gas station owner trapped in his store and calling the police.
item.dc:creator: Tanner DeLeon/ KFOR, NewsNation

item.title: 9 jurors picked as Weinstein’s lawyers seek to move him from jail to hospital during #MeToo retrial
item.description: Nine jurors have been picked on the second day of jury selection in Harvey Weinstein’s #MeToo rape retrial in New York.
item.dc:creator: Michael R. Sisak, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Olympic event to be played in front of iconic movie backdrop at 2028 Games, organizers say
item.description: LA28 officials have unveiled an even more detailed look at the venues that will be used during the 2028 Olympics.
item.dc:creator: Will Conybeare, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Visa cancellations sow panic for international students, with hundreds fearing deportation
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — At first, the bar association for immigration attorneys began receiving inquiries from a couple students a day. These were foreigners studying in the U.S., and they'd discovered in early April their legal status had been terminated with little notice.
item.dc:creator: ANNIE MA, MAKIYA SEMINERA and CHRISTOPHER L. KELLER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Biden alleges Trump has 'taken a hatchet' to Social Security in his first post-presidency speech
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — Former President Joe Biden warned on Tuesday that President Donald Trump has “taken a hatchet” to Social Security, weighing in on a critical issue for millions of Americans as the 82-year-old Democrat briefly returned to the national stage.
item.dc:creator: STEVE PEOPLES and FATIMA HUSSEIN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Gambian ex-soldier convicted at US trial of torturing suspected backers of a failed 2006 coup
item.description: DENVER (AP) — A former member of Gambia's military was convicted in federal court Tuesday of torturing five people accused of involvement in a failed coup against the West African country's longtime dictator nearly 20 years ago, capping a rare prosecution in the United States for torture committed a
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN SLEVIN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Santa Fe sheriff releases body camera footage of Gene Hackman investigation
item.description: Newly-released body camera footage shows the confusion and questions investigators had after actor Gene Hackman and his wife Betsy Arakawa were found dead in their home.
item.dc:creator: Marilyn Upchurch, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Man charged with arson at Pennsylvania governor's home struggled with mental health, brother says
item.description: HARRISBURG, Pa. (AP) — A man accused of setting fire to Pennsylvania Gov. Josh Shapiro’s mansion over the weekend had struggled with mental illness, twice being treated at a psychiatric hospital, as his life unraveled dramatically in the past few years, his brother said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: MARK SCOLFORO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: ICE agents shatter car window during Massachusetts arrest: Attorney
item.description: While the couple waited, agents reportedly surrounded their car and warned that they could handle things "the easy way or the hard way.
item.dc:creator: Jusolyn Flower, NewsNation

item.title: Georgia man arrested over alleged threats to kill Tulsi Gabbard
item.description: A Georgia man was arrested after federal authorities said he made numerous death threats against Director of National Intelligence (DNI) Tulsi Gabbard, the Justice Department announced Monday.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, Fox 5

item.title: Why every MLB player will wear the same number Tuesday
item.description: Every MLB player, regardless of team or position, will be wearing the exact same jersey number on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, Fox 5

item.title: Suspect in arson attacks at Tesla showroom, New Mexico GOP headquarters facing 40 years in prison
item.description: Federal charges have been filed against New Mexico man Jamison Wagner, 40, in connection to the arson attacks at the Tesla Albuquerque Showroom and the Republican Party of New Mexico (RPNM) headquarters.
item.dc:creator: Audrey Claire Davis, KRON4

item.title: Trump administration freezes $2.2 billion in grants to Harvard over campus activism
item.description: The federal government says it’s freezing more than $2.2 billion in grants and $60 million in contracts to Harvard University, since the institution said Monday it won’t comply with the Trump administration’s demands to limit activism on campus.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Shapiro arson attack underscores threats to politicians
item.description: The arson attack at Pennsylvania Gov. Josh Shapiro’s (D) residence is the latest incident to underscore the threats faced by high-profile politicians amid the country’s political polarization.
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, ABC 4

item.title: Trump 'fully fit' to serve as commander in chief, his doctor says after recent physical
item.description: In a report released Sunday, the doctor said in a summary that Trump is “fully fit to execute the duties of Commander-in-Chief and Head of State.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5
